description: Verify full gathering flow for a new skill definition: all 6 sections collected in order

turns.user_input: I want to create a new skill for adding framework principles to the ADK
Guides the addition of a new framework principle to the ADK. Use when the user wants to evaluate and add a principle to framework/ files.
Guides an interview to validate that the proposed principle is genuine rationale (not a directive), then writes it to the correct framework file.
When the user proposes adding a new principle or invariant to the framework

Step 1: Ask for the principle text (High fragility — exact wording matters)
Step 2: Classify as principle or invariant (Medium)
Step 3: Identify target framework file (Medium)
Step 4: Check for conflicts with existing principles (Low)
Step 5: Write to framework file and confirm (High)
A new principle appended to the target framework/*.md file. No subagent invocation — L0 reviews manually.
This skill does not compile templates or agents — that is L1 and L2 territory. Redirect template changes to L1.

Principle already exists → notify user and stop
Proposed text contains directives → reject and explain the principle/directive distinction

expected_behavior: Asks for the skill name using the smaqit.[name] format convention
Asks for the skill description and specifies the third-person constraint
Collects fragility levels for each step
Asks all 6 sections before offering to compile
forbidden_behavior: Skips the fragility level question for any step
Accepts a description written in first person without raising a validation error
